body.title: [sprint] Clear-to-base context between tickets via native /rewind (keep base + commits, excise ticket delta)
body.description:
**Problem:** A builder working ticket-after-ticket accumulates each ticket's context delta on top of a large static base (everything /context-load brings — rules, palace, slate, substrate). Treating context as all-or-nothing forces a bad choice: re-run /context-load every ticket (re-paying tokens for the static base) or let the window grow unbounded. Mid-ticket compaction also strands uncommitted work because state lives in context, not durably. Root: External State Principle + no "bookmark base / clear to base" primitive in the builder loop. NOTE: ticket id is legacy ("per-ticket-checkpoint"); the mechanism is now clear-to-base via native rewind, not a checkpoint file.

**Proposed shape:** A `clear_to_base` step the builder runs BETWEEN tickets, built on Claude Code's NATIVE conversation-only rewind:
1. Run /context-load ONCE; the prompt right after it is the BASE restore-point (the bookmark).
2. Build the ticket (edits + git commit).
3. clear_to_base: drive `/rewind` -> "Restore conversation" back to base via the existing tmux send-keys machinery (same pattern as uucompactclaude drives /compact). This excises ONLY the ticket's conversation delta; the base stays in the window (NEVER re-tokenized, NEVER re-loaded) and code + git commits are untouched.
4. Next ticket starts from the clean base.

WHY this works (claude-code-guide research, 2026-06-16): CC's `/rewind` -> "Restore conversation" rewinds the conversation while keeping current code; checkpointing tracks only Edit/Write tool edits, NEVER bash `git commit`, so commits are automatically safe. This REPLACES the wrong primitive (the original plan's lossy /compact summarize + selective preserve string + rehydrate -- `/compact` is a lossy AI summary, confirmed, not clean truncation). Programmatic transcript (JSONL) truncation is UNSUPPORTED -- do NOT hand-edit it. Hooks CANNOT trigger /rewind, so send-keys is the driver, not a hook.

Fork-per-ticket (`claude --resume <base> --fork-session`) was evaluated as a second selectable backend and DELIBERATELY DEFERRED (Akien, 2026-06-16): higher risk -- it spawns a CC process per ticket, colliding with the never-spawn-a-CC + one-CC-at-a-time guardrails (the 2026-06-01 runaway shape) -- for a benefit we cannot yet articulate. Recorded in D-architecture-as-code-cognition-pipeline-2026-06-16 so the rationale survives to the next look at this code. No mode selector is built (single mechanism for now).

**Affected files:**
- NEW: lab/claudecode/uurewindtobase (or similar) -- tmux send-keys driver, sibling to uucompactclaude, but navigates the /rewind MENU (select base entry + "Restore conversation") instead of sending one command.
- NEW or skills/clear-to-base -- the builder's between-ticket step that invokes the driver. (skills/autocompact's block-end summarize stays as-is for CC.0/day-close; clear-to-base is the per-ticket BUILDER step, a different thing.)
- skills/sprint-ticket entry -- invoke clear_to_base after a ticket closes (the between-ticket boundary).
- base-bookmark recorder -- capture/identify the base restore-point (prompt right after /context-load) and store the marker durably (External State) so the menu drive selects it deterministically across the session.
- (reference only, NO change) devices/granny/* dispatch -- the builder stays ONE session; Granny still send-keys "ticket waiting". No new process. This is exactly why rewind fits and fork did not.
- lab/claudecode/uucompactclaude -- reference template for the send-keys driver.

**Design rules:** External State Principle (the base bookmark marker held durably, not only in context); CC concurrency hard limit (rewind keeps it ONE session, no spawn -- the reason fork was rejected); do-not hand-edit the session JSONL (unsupported -- use the native menu).

**Scope boundary:**
IN -- (1) bookmark the base (post-/context-load point); (2) a clear_to_base step driven by tmux send-keys running `/rewind` -> "Restore conversation" to base between tickets; (3) deterministic menu navigation to the base entry; (4) verify code + git commits survive the rewind.
OUT -- fork backend (deferred, higher risk -- rationale in the decision doc); any mode selector (single mechanism); the CC.0/CC.1 role split itself (Granny dispatch / availability / daemon arch -- still narrative, needs Akien); the durable per-ticket work-state FILE (native rewind keeps code+commits, so the original checkpoint-file is unnecessary in v1 -- add later ONLY if a real interrupted-mid-ticket case demands it); programmatic transcript truncation (unsupported).

**Test plan:**
- Bookmark + clear: run /context-load (base), build a ticket (edits + a git commit), run clear_to_base -> conversation rewinds to the base prompt; the ticket's chat delta is gone; the git commit and working files REMAIN (verify `git log` + file presence).
- Token check (the whole point): after clear_to_base the base is NOT re-loaded/re-executed and NOT re-tokenized -- /context-load did not re-run; the base content was not re-sent (the native menu rewind keeps the existing base in-window).
- Determinism: across N consecutive tickets, clear_to_base reliably selects the SAME base entry (menu navigation stable as history grows).
- Regression: a normal block-end /autocompact (CC.0/day-close) still works unchanged.

**Completion criteria:**
- A base restore-point is bookmarked at/after /context-load and identified deterministically.
- clear_to_base drives `/rewind` -> "Restore conversation" to base via tmux send-keys (no hook, no JSONL surgery, no CC spawn).
- After clear_to_base: ticket conversation delta excised; base intact in-window (not re-tokenized); code + git commits preserved (verified via `git log` + working tree).
- Menu navigation selects the base entry deterministically across consecutive tickets.
- Fork backend NOT implemented (deliberately deferred -- rationale in D-architecture-as-code-cognition-pipeline-2026-06-16).
- Block-end /autocompact still fires cleanly (regression).
body.intention: I intend that a builder working ticket-after-ticket pays for the static base context once, bookmarks it, and clears back to that base between tickets via native conversation-only /rewind -- excising only the ticket's delta while keeping code and git commits -- so context neither re-loads/re-tokenizes the base nor grows unbounded. Fork backend deferred (spawn-CC risk).
